data.content:
This tombstone does not contain any .NET runtime frames. The backtrace consists of:

- **`libc.so`** — Android system C library (`strlen`)
- **`libutils.so`** — Android system utility library
- **`libnativeapp.so`** — The app's own native JNI library
- **`libart.so`** — Android **ART** (Java/Kotlin) runtime

The app (`com.example.nativeapp`) is a pure Java/Kotlin + native (JNI) app. There is no `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, or any other .NET library in the stack. There are no .NET frames to symbolicate.
data.reasoningText:
This tombstone file shows a crash in a native Android app (`com.example.nativeapp`). Looking at the backtrace:

1. Frame #00: `libc.so` - system library (strlen)
2. Frame #01: `libutils.so` - system library (String8::set)
3. Frame #02: `libnativeapp.so` - the app's native library (Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData)
4. Frame #03-#05: `libart.so` - Android ART runtime

There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone. The app is `com.example.nativeapp` - a purely native Android app using ART (Android Runtime, i.e., Java/Kotlin). There's no `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, or any other .NET runtime library in the backtrace.


This means I can't help symbolicate this crash since it's not a .NET application.
